title: Chicken Lentil Soup
excerpt: Ingredients 1 large onion, finely chopped 30 ml sunflower oil 15 ml curry powder 10 ml turmeric 750 ml boiling water 250 ml pumpkin, coarsely grated 250 ml sweet potato, coarsely grated 125 ml red lentils 2 chicken stock cubes 2 chicken fillets, cut into 2-cm cubes for serving: plain yoghurt, sour cream or coconut cream and coarsely chopped coriander leaves Method Fry …
html:
Ingredients

  • 1 large onion, finely chopped
  • 30 ml sunflower oil
  • 15 ml curry powder
  • 10 ml turmeric
  • 750 ml boiling water
  • 250 ml pumpkin, coarsely grated
  • 250 ml sweet potato, coarsely grated
  • 125 ml red lentils
  • 2 chicken stock cubes
  • 2 chicken fillets, cut into 2-cm cubes
  • for serving: plain yoghurt, sour cream or coconut cream and coarsely chopped coriander leaves

Method

Fry the onion lightly brown in the oil and add the curry powder and turmeric. Stir until fragrant and add the boiling water, pumpkin, sweet potato and lentils. Bring to the boil, lower the heat and simmer for 15 minutes or until the lentils start to soften.

Crumble the stock cubes into the soup and stir in well. Now add the chicken and cook for a further 5 – 8 minutes or until the chicken is done. Let the soup cool enough that you can blend it with a potato masher or a food processor.

Serve with a dollop of yoghurt, sour cream or coconut cream and coriander leaves.

Serves 4 with fresh bread and butter.

Easy!
